summary: Iteration 6 backend-only testing. Verified Easyship replaced Shiptheory as fallback rate provider, store-credit closes returns (closed=true, closed_reason='store_credit_applied'), mark-refunded closes returns (closed_reason='refunded'), existing-items still reports items from closed store-credit returns so the same line item can't re-apply, and the 'Store credit has already been applied' 409 branch exists in server.py. 10/10 tests passed against the external preview URL.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1814 lines — consider splitting admin/returns/customer routes into separate routers for maintainability (not a blocker).
integrations_ping.test_all imports easyship_service locally inside the function (line 105). Works fine but top-level import would be cleaner and consistent with the rest.

Response shape of GET /api/admin/settings and PUT /api/admin/settings returns flat settings object (contains easyship_api_key, easyship_api_key_set, easyship_api_key_preview). Tests pass with flat shape. Good.

Store-credit conflict branch at server.py:305-318 correctly returns HTTP 409 with message starting with 'Store credit has already been applied for: ...'. Reachable only via real Woo flow — verified by code path + the existing-items persistence test which exercises the same DB query.
mark-refunded endpoint at server.py:1782 now sets closed=true/closed_reason='refunded'/closed_at alongside refunded=true and status='refunded' — verified by MongoDB round-trip test.
test_credentials: admin@pgelimited.com / admin123 (from /app/backend/.env). /app/memory/test_credentials.md is present but empty — flagged for future runs.
seed_data_creation: Inserted and cleaned up two temporary returns docs: id='r-sc-test-iter6' (order TEST-SC-999) and id='r-mkref-test-iter6' (order TEST-MKREF-1). Also persisted easyship_api_key='test_eas_abc123xyz' in app_settings for the persistence test — this is a dummy test key and is intentionally left in the settings doc (masked in responses).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_iter6_easyship_storecredit.py. Run with: cd /app/backend && pytest tests/test_iter6_easyship_storecredit.py -v. Uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from env. MongoDB writes to test_database.returns and app_settings. Woo/Stripe/Shippo keys are intentionally unset in dev — their connection-test results are expected to show ok=false; that is not a regression. Only 'easyship' key presence is verified in POST /api/admin/settings/test.
